seoTitle: How to Connect Power BI With SAP Step by Step
metaDescription: Connect Power BI to SAP HANA or SAP Business Warehouse, from installing required drivers to navigating the Data Navigator and publishing dashboards.
intro: Pulling enterprise SAP data into Power BI opens up consolidated financial and supply chain reporting, but the setup involves a few prerequisite steps most people miss the first time. This guide covers installing the necessary drivers and connecting to SAP HANA or Business Warehouse so you can build dashboards directly on top of your SAP data.

steps.heading: Install required driver packages
steps.body: Before connecting, install the mandatory local .NET driver packages Power BI needs to communicate with SAP systems.

steps.heading: Choose your SAP connection type
steps.body: Decide whether you're connecting to SAP HANA or SAP Business Warehouse, since Power BI provides dedicated native connectors for each.

steps.heading: Enter your server connection details
steps.body: Provide the correct server address and authentication details for your SAP instance inside the Power BI connection dialog.

steps.heading: Navigate the Data Navigator pane
steps.body: Use the multi-table Data Navigator to select the specific SAP tables or views you want to import into your data model.

steps.heading: Publish and schedule refresh
steps.body: Once your model is built, publish it to your cloud workspace and configure automated scheduled refreshes to keep the SAP data current.

faq.question: Do I need special drivers to connect Power BI to SAP?
faq.answer: Yes, you typically need to install the required .NET driver packages before Power BI can establish a connection to SAP systems.

faq.question: Can I connect to both SAP HANA and SAP BW?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI includes native connectors for both SAP HANA and SAP Business Warehouse.

faq.question: Why is my SAP connection slow?
faq.answer: Version mismatches between drivers and your SAP instance, or querying overly large tables, are common causes of slow performance.

faq.question: Do I need a gateway to refresh SAP data in the cloud?
faq.answer: Yes, since SAP systems are typically on-premises, a data gateway is generally required to support scheduled cloud refreshes.
